chapters.title: Mrs. Hinch has turned the “boredom” of cleaning into an enjoyable habit
chapters.text:
For many people, cleaning is a chore. It is something that needs to be done and isn’t necessarily enjoyed, but it’s endured in many ways. However, Mrs. Hinch has turned all of that on its head! With her endearing, upbeat personality and the way she gives all of her cleaning apparatus names, Mrs. Hinch is racking up Instagram followers by the millions. She calls cleaning “hinching,” and she loves to hinch most days!

Sophie Hinchliffe lives in Essex with her husband, children, and their pet dog, Henry Hinch. She wants to stress that she’s not a professional cleaner, but she enjoys the process of taking something dirty and dusty and making it shiny and clean once more. She also believes that if you have a clean and tidy home that you’re proud of, it helps to keep your mind clean and tidy too.

Your home should be the one place you can retire to at the end of a long day and feel completely comfortable. Keeping it clean helps you to relax in your favorite space.

Mrs. Hinch admits that for a long time, she was too concerned about what other people thought. She would design her home to gain admiration and respect, but nowadays, she focuses upon what she likes. You should not design your home for other people, and it should never be a “show home.” As long as it is clean and you feel comfortable there, your home is your sanctuary.

If you’re someone who has to force themselves to grab the duster and start cleaning, Hinch Yourself Happy will help you see cleaning as an enjoyable task. By the end, you might even start giving your dusters and cleaning equipment names of their own too!

Did you know? Before her social media success, Mrs. Hinch was a hairdresser.

chapters.title: Everyone deals with worries in their own way. How about cleaning?
chapters.text:
Mrs. Hinch admits to struggling with anxiety from time to time, and she often takes small worries and turns them into significant issues. This is something that many people can sympathize with, but everyone deals with their worries in their way. For example, some people turn to unhealthy coping mechanisms, such as smoking or drinking too much, but Mrs. Hinch directs her attention to cleaning instead.

Whenever she feels her worries start to mount up, Mrs. Hinch grabs her favorite cleaning products and gets to work. The process of cleaning something to the point of shining helps distract her mind, and she also has a huge sense of achievement at the end. Is this something that you could use to help manage your worries and problems?

Some people go to the gym or they bake cakes, and that’s what helps to ease their whirring minds. For me, it’s cleaning. ~ Mrs. Hinch

Organizing your home and making sure that everything is clean is a far better way to distract yourself from worries, compared to spending too much, drinking, overeating, and smoking. Once you’ve cleaned the house, you’ll feel like you’ve done something productive with your time, and your worries will seem less troublesome.

Mrs. Hinch suggests thinking about your favorite scents and looking into candles or wax melts too. This can help to create a relaxing space to spend your time. There’s nothing better than a deliciously smelling house once it’s all clean and tidy!

Distracting your mind is a great way to handle anxiety. The fact that you forgot about your worries for a short time helps you to see that they’re not all that important in the end.

chapters.title: Motivate yourself by picturing a shiny, clean house
chapters.text:
Many people detest cleaning. They see it as nothing more than something they have to do but do not enjoy at all. Mrs. Hinch suggests you try thinking about cleaning differently. Rather than looking at the mess in front of you and how long it’s going to take you to clean it up, think about the result and how great it will feel. For many people, that’s motivation to get started and keep going until the picture in your mind matches what you see in front of you.

You don’t have to have a set cleaning schedule. You don’t have to do a full clean every single day or even every couple of days. Instead, look around your house daily and do what needs to be done. By giving yourself a strict schedule, you’re putting pressure on your cleaning needs, and you’re making it less enjoyable.

Cleaning can be a chore, but it doesn’t have to feel like one. Imagine how you want your home to look and then get to work creating what you see in your mind’s eye.

Mrs. Hinch vacuums, dusts, and cleans the floors every day. She also sprays the toilet down and wipes her kitchen surfaces. If anything else looks like it needs to be done, she will do so, but there is no set schedule. Her bathroom and bins are thoroughly cleaned every week, and the beds are changed. She will also clean her oven and microwave every couple of weeks and the cupboards in the kitchen every month.

Many things in your house do not need regular cleaning, but keeping on top of small cleaning tasks, such as vacuuming or wiping down the kitchen surfaces, will avoid a significant grime build-up.

To make your cleaning session more fun, put some music on and dance around. Mrs. Hinch is a big Beyonce fan when she’s cleaning!

chapters.title: Try Mrs. Hinch’s favorite cleaning tips for yourself
chapters.text:
Mrs. Hinch has a world of different tips for cleaning, or hinching. Of course, everyone has their own specific ways of cleaning their house, but perhaps you would like some of her hinching tips for yourself. You never know, you may find a new way that suits you far better that you enjoy more too.

Listen to cleaning tips but always go with the way that suits you best. Cleaning has to be your hobby and something you enjoy, not a set of instructions to follow!

One of the most satisfying rooms of the house to clean is the bathroom. Once it’s finished, the entire room will sparkle. Mrs. Hinch suggests using a dedicated bathroom spray and moving everything that can be taken out of the room. Spray everything down and leave for a few minutes before rinsing clean. Make sure you spray your shower head, taps, and plugs to ensure that limescale doesn’t cause dullness. Be sure to clean your shelving and surfaces too, and then dowse everything in water and rinse thoroughly. Clean the toilet down carefully, starting with the lid, and then mop the floors. The bathroom is done!

The kitchen fridge is also somewhere you should pay a lot of attention. Make sure that you do a regular empty and get rid of anything out of date or spoiled. Remove the baskets and soak them in dish soap for a short while. Wipe everything down with a suitable cleaner and allow it to dry before you put everything back inside.

Mrs. Hinch also really enjoys cleaning her washing machine.

I hinch my washing machine regularly. Come on, it takes care of our clothes and we should take care of it back. ~ Mrs. Hinch

If your washing machine has drawers, remove them and allow them to soak clean. Spray inside where the drawers were and use a cloth to get inside, where a lot of built-up grime can dwell. Pull down the rim of the washing machine and spray, giving it a really good clean as, again, this is where a lot of dirt builds up. You should then clean the window with glass cleaner and rinse out the drawers before putting everything back together.

Cleaning the inside should also be done monthly. Mrs. Hinch suggests using three tablespoons of bicarbonate of soda and a cup of white vinegar and then running a 90-minute cycle.

The entire house is full of machinery and spaces that need to be hinched carefully. Prioritize the most important ones and don’t make a rigid schedule of it.

chapters.title: Remember, health and safety matter too!
chapters.text:
Just because your home is your sanctuary, that doesn’t mean it can’t be a dangerous place too! Cleaning products contain chemicals that can be harmful if ingested accidentally or handled by children. Mrs. Hinch makes health and safety a crucial part of her cleaning routine and gives you some tips on how to avoid costly mistakes while cleaning your home and storing products.

Pay attention to your smoke detectors.

Fire alarms and smoke detectors need to be serviced regularly, but you should also check them monthly to ensure that batteries are working.

Use different cleaning cloths for different rooms.

Bacteria and grime can be found everywhere, and you don’t want to move the nasty stuff you find in the toilet into the kitchen! Use different cloths for each room and wash them after every use.

Store your cleaning products carefully.

This is even more important if you have children in the house. Most people tend to store their cleaning products in the bathroom, utility room, or even in a lower kitchen cupboard. You either need to use child safety locks on your cupboard or store them in a place where little hands can’t reach them. Of course, you also need to think about pet safety too!

Remember that all cleaning products are different.

If you happen to buy a cleaning product you’ve never used before, read the instructions and follow them carefully. Some products interact badly with others.

When you’re cleaning and moving furniture out of the way, be careful that you don’t trip and fall. This is especially important if you’re listening to music and dancing around while cleaning!

Cleaning involves chemicals, and that means you need to be mindful of who touches them and where you store them. Have a dedicated space and make sure there is a lock on the door to keep visitors out.

chapters.title: Declutter to clear your home and your mind
chapters.text:
Decluttering your home can be a great way to clear your mind and reduce stress. There is something very satisfying about clearing out a cupboard and having more space to play with. Every few months, Mrs. Hinch has a regular house declutter, looking in cabinets and drawers to ensure nothing unnecessary is sitting there.

If you have similar spaces in your home where junk tends to accumulate, pull everything out and set it into piles. You need a pile for the things you want to keep, which will then be organized back in the cupboard more tidily. You also need a pile for the things you don’t want. Those items can then be sorted into throw-away or gifting piles. Of course, remember to recycle anything you can and make sure that you’re not needlessly using too much plastic in your storage.

When decluttering, make sure that you’re ruthless in making the most of your efforts.

If you have a garage, make sure you declutter in there occasionally too. Be sure to make the space as pleasant as you can; Mrs. Hinch often hangs pictures on the garage walls. Maximize your garage space by making use of the walls. For example, try bike racks or old drawers for storage and shelving.

Your wardrobe is another key decluttering spot. Mrs. Hinch rotates her clothing by season and puts the clothes not in use in vacuum bags. Have a big clear out every year but also have a smaller declutter every few months. Again, be ruthless! If you haven’t worn something for a year, you should declutter it. If it’s old or in bad condition, away it goes.

Don’t worry about giving old or broken clothes to clothing banks as these can often be fixed and put to great use.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Cleaning doesn’t have to be one of those tasks that you keep putting off until another day. You can make the entire task fun and become addicted to keeping your house shiny and clean, just like Mrs. Hinch! You don’t have to go the lengths of calling all your cloths and dusters by their own names, but if you want to, go for it. As long as you enjoy your cleaning endeavors, that’s all that matters.

Every single house needs to be cleaned regularly. It’s not only about having a space that is enjoyable and looks good, but it’s also about health and safety. Allowing too much dirt and dust to build up can be dangerous for health, and when you have too much clutter in your space, it becomes a hazard for trips and falls.

We spend a considerable amount of time in our homes, and it stands to reason that we should want our space to be something we’re proud of and where we enjoy spending time. Help reduce the stress and anxiety in your life by cleaning and enjoying the process and creating a space that you can retreat to at the end of a long day.

Whether you dedicate a day per week to your entire house deep clean or you follow Mrs. Hinch’s advice and just do the tasks when they need doing, it doesn’t matter. Throw on some music, imagine how you want the space to look, and get to work. At the end of your hinching session, you’ll have a home you want to spend more time in, free of grime, germs, and dust.

Try this:
• Imagine how you really want your home to look. Do you need to buy some new furniture or change your bedding? Create your ideal space, and you’ll be more motivated to keep it clean.
• Buy some wicker baskets to keep all your cleaning equipment in, and make sure that you store them in a lockable cupboard. If you don’t have child safety locks yet, now is the time to get them.
• Grab your phone and create a cleaning playlist. Dance around while you clean; it’s exercise too!

Read it till the end?
